title: Vilfa coromandeliana
intro: Vilfa coromandeliana, also known as Vilfa or Coromandel grass, is a type of plant belonging to the family Poaceae, commonly found in the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Vilfa coromandeliana contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, and is also used in traditional medicine to make formulations such as tablets and capsules.
taxonomy: Vilfa coromandeliana is classified as a species of grass within the genus Vilfa, which belongs to the family Poaceae. The genus Vilfa is part of the subfamily Pooideae. The family Poaceae, also known as the grass family, is one of the largest and most widespread families of flowering plants. The subfamily Pooideae is a group of cool-season grasses that are commonly found in temperate regions. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and molecular characteristics.
